response:
Sirirag Mahala 3॥ Gurmukh kripa kare bhagat kiji bin Gur bhagat na hoie॥ Aapai aap milaaye bujhaye ta nirmal ho vai soi॥ Har jeeo sachaa sachee baanee sabad milaava hoie॥1॥ Bhai re bhagathee na kahae jag aaeia॥ Pure Gur ki sev na keeni birtha janam gavaeia॥1॥ Rahaou॥ Aape jagjeevan sukhdaataa aape bakhsh milaaye॥ Jeeo jant e kia vechaare kia ko aakh sunaaye॥ Gurmukh aape dey vadaaee aape sev karaaye॥2॥ Dekh kutamb mohi lobhaanaa chaladia naal na jaaeia॥ Satgur sevi gun nidhaan paayia tis dee keem na paayia॥ Har prabh shakha meet prabh meraa antae hoei shakhaaee॥3॥ Aapnae man chit kahai kahaee bin Gur aap na jaaeia॥ Har jeeo daataa bhagat vachal hai kar kripa man vasaaye॥ Nanak sobhaa surat dey prabh aape Gurmukh de vadaaee॥4॥15॥48॥

***  
The gene of spiritual language is deeply rooted in the spirit of visit and devotion. This spirit connects all beings through the sacred words of the Guru. The language of the spirit transcends the physical and invites a visit to the inner self. Gene and spirit are intertwined in the journey of the soul. Language becomes a bridge for the spirit to express its truth. Visit the heart and find the meaning of life through the teachings passed down. Spirit guides the gene of every being toward enlightenment. Language, spirit, and visit are essential in the path of spiritual awakening.
